notable_shots: 3P: 'Wrote about my daily-driver agent for @every, and open-sourced the plugin' — product + proof + value
MID: 'Testing Claude Code at Every to automate growth ops' — shows process, implies capability
FT: 'Missing the old ESPN NBA Countdown crew' — pure nostalgia, humanizes the growth lead
AST: Retweeted Dan's Spiral workflow thread with commentary on agent adoption
AIR: Generic 'Excited for this week at Every' with no specific hook
twitter.walkway_notes: Austin's agent experiment tweets aren't promos — they're proof-of-work. When he open-sources a tool (GitHub) and tweets the story (Twitter), the attribution flows naturally: 'This person builds real things' → 'Every builds real things'. His ESPN nostalgia creates the human floor that makes his tech tweets land harder.

linkedin.opportunity: LinkedIn is where enterprise buyers evaluate the Head of Growth. 3 posts in 90 days is a ghost town. A monthly 'What AI agents taught me about subscription growth' post would bridge ESPN media strategy → Every growth strategy.

instagram.opportunity: Not a visual storyteller by habit. Skip unless product demos demand it.

product_hunt.opportunity: As Head of Growth, Austin should own the Product Hunt launch strategy. Every product launch on PH is a high-intent discovery surface. A personal maker profile would add credibility to future launches.

surface_iq_summary.superpower: Efficient Volume — low usage, high trust conversion
surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Increase walk frequency without increasing walk intensity. One thoughtful tweet per week beats five generic ones. The Montaigne post took 20 minutes and generated more profile visits than a month of casual tweets.
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: Restraint is a weapon. Austin proves that rare, high-effort posts outperform daily noise. The team learns: when you do speak, make it count.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: NEWSLETTER: A quarterly 'What I Learned Running Growth at Every' would be the most-read essay in the builder community. The content exists in his head; the surface doesn't.

growth_loop_position.attribution_mechanism: Technical proof-of-work (GitHub) + narrative proof (Twitter) creates the 'Every builds real tools' conviction that converts to product curiosity
growth_loop_position.compound_insight: Austin's agent experiments are Every's best marketing for 'agent-native operations' because they demonstrate rather than declare.

notable_shots: 3P: 'I used Spiral to outline this essay — here's how' — product demo wrapped in process
MID: 'The real risk of AI isn't replacement — it's autopilot' — philosophy that positions Every's editorial stance
FT: 'Current reading list: Proust, Murakami, and the NBA playoffs' — pure humanity, zero work
AST: Boosted Kate's editorial thread on writing craft with personal commentary
AIR: Generic 'Great week at Every!' — rare for Dan, but happens under fatigue
twitter.walkway_notes: Dan's personal tweets (reading lists, philosophy, founder life) create the gravity field. His product tweets land at 2x the rate they would otherwise because the audience trusts the person first. The walkway is: personal conviction → editorial authority → product curiosity. When Dan tweets about Proust and the NBA in the same breath, it signals 'this is a human building for humans' — which is exactly why people subscribe to Every.

linkedin.opportunity: LinkedIn is a ghost town for Dan. The newsletter and podcast already own long-form authority. But: enterprise consulting and Spiral for Teams narratives live on LinkedIn. A quarterly 'What we're seeing' post could bridge media → enterprise without becoming a content calendar.

instagram.opportunity: Minimal presence. Occasional personal/lifestyle posts humanize the CEO. Not a growth surface, but a trust surface.

product_hunt.opportunity: Dan is the face of Every. Product Hunt launches with his maker profile attached would drive 2-3x more upvotes than anonymous brand posts. The CEO as maker is a proven PH pattern.

surface_iq_summary.superpower: Narrative Spacing — personal tweets create gravity for product tweets
surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Dan doesn't need to post more. He needs to know that his SPACING is the product. Every personal tweet is a screen that makes the next product tweet open. The danger is overcrowding the floor — if 3P% creeps above 15%, TS% collapses.
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: Your personal voice is the spacing. When you tweet about what you're reading or thinking without a product mention, you create floor space for your product tweets to actually land. The team learns: restraint isn't silence; it's strategy.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: LINKEDIN: Dan's enterprise narrative is underdeveloped. One post per quarter on 'What AI adoption looks like at 50 companies' would unlock consulting conversations that Twitter never will.

growth_loop_position.attribution_mechanism: Personal philosophy → editorial authority → newsletter subscription → product usage. Dan's voice IS the top of the funnel.
growth_loop_position.compound_insight: Dan's reading lists and philosophy tweets have a half-life of years. They continue generating follows and subscriptions long after posting because they signal taste — and taste is the scarcest resource in AI.

notable_shots: 3P: 'Join me this Friday for the Cora launch' — direct CTA, low engagement (5 likes)
MID: 'I am more and more convinced that this is the future of software development UI' — 315 likes, 43 replies. Conviction tweet.
FT: 'Weekend hack project with the kids' — rare personal, high trust
AST: 'Dan's piece on agent UI is the best thing I've read this month' — boosting editorial + positioning own expertise
AIR: Rare — Kieran's airball rate is near zero
twitter.walkway_notes: Kieran's conviction tweets about agent UI aren't selling Cora — they're EXPLAINING it. When the GM of Cora tweets 'this is the future of software UI,' he's not asking for a sale; he's sharing a worldview. The audience absorbs that worldview and associates it with Cora. When he then shoots a 3 ('Join the Cora launch'), it lands softer because the trust is already banked — but the model predicts his 3P% would spike if he front-loaded with 2 mid-range vision tweets first.

linkedin.opportunity: LinkedIn is where Cora's enterprise buyers evaluate technical leadership. 6 posts in 90 days misses the window. A bi-weekly 'Agent UI Pattern' post would establish Kieran as the category thinker.

instagram.opportunity: Skip. Cora is a developer tool. Instagram is not a dev surface.

product_hunt.opportunity: Cora has a Product Hunt page but Kieran isn't listed as a maker. For developer tools, the GM/founder as maker is table stakes. This is a credibility leak.

surface_iq_summary.superpower: Conviction Spacing — philosophy tweets explain the product better than product tweets
surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Kieran's 3P% is higher when he's already made two mid-range passes. The growth loop insight: conviction before conversion. His Twitter is already doing the hardest work. The missing piece is capturing that conviction in a durable format (newsletter, GitHub README) that compounds beyond the tweet lifespan.
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: The GM's personal conviction is the product marketing. For technical products, the founder/GM's philosophy IS the brand. The team learns: explain why you built it before you ask people to try it.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: NEWSLETTER: A 'Cora Design Diary' substack would attract the exact developer audience that needs to trust the product before they try it. The content is already in his tweets; it just needs a home.

growth_loop_position.attribution_mechanism: Technical vision (Twitter) + code proof (GitHub) creates the 'Cora is built by someone who sees the future' conviction that converts developers
growth_loop_position.compound_insight: Kieran's agent UI tweets have a 6-month half-life. Developers bookmark them, reference them in meetings, and eventually try Cora because they trust the vision.

notable_shots: 3P: 'We burned nearly a billion tokens so your desktop doesn't have to look like a crime scene anymore' — product promo wrapped in personality. High difficulty, high memorability.
MID: 'The file system is broken and nobody wants to admit it' — domain expertise that positions Sparkle's thesis
FT: 'Mumbai street food > SF sourdough, fight me' — pure personality, zero work
AST: 'Kieran's agent UI thread is exactly why we built Sparkle the way we did' — connecting teammate content to product philosophy
AIR: Generic product update with screenshot but no narrative hook
twitter.walkway_notes: Yash's step-back 3 is the highest-difficulty shot on the roster. He wraps product mentions in voice, humor, and technical proof. The 'billion tokens' tweet isn't a CTA — it's a flex. That flex creates the 'Sparkle is built by people who ship' attribution. His personal tweets (food, life) create the warmth that makes the flex feel earned, not arrogant.

linkedin.opportunity: LinkedIn is underutilized. Sparkle is a productivity tool for knowledge workers — exactly the LinkedIn audience. A monthly 'Desktop Archaeology' post (what your file system says about your workflow) would drive organic discovery.

instagram.opportunity: Sparkle is a VISUAL product. A 15-second 'before/after desktop cleanup' Reel is the most obvious viral format in the world. The product is visual; the surface is waiting.

product_hunt.opportunity: Sparkle is on PH but Yash isn't attached as maker. His personality-driven approach would crush on PH — 'We burned a billion tokens' is literally PH headline material.

surface_iq_summary.superpower: Product-as-Personality — promo wrapped in voice, humor, and technical proof
surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Yash has the highest 'shot difficulty' but also the highest 'shot memorability.' The growth loop insight: personality scales. When the GM's voice is the product voice, every tweet is a product demo. The missing piece is visual proof (Instagram/TikTok) for a visual product.
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: Promo doesn't have to be boring. Yash shows that the best growth content is indistinguishable from the founder's personality. The team learns: even your product tweets can be YOU.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: INSTAGRAM/TIKTOK: Sparkle is a desktop product. A 'Desktop Archaeology' series showing messy → clean transformations would be pure viral fuel. The GM should be the face of it.

growth_loop_position.attribution_mechanism: Personality-driven tweets create the 'Sparkle is built by humans with taste' signal that converts to product trials
growth_loop_position.compound_insight: Yash's voice is Sparkle's brand. When he tweets about food, culture, and life, he's not off-topic — he's proving that the people building your productivity tool actually have lives. That humanity is the differentiator.

notable_shots: 3P: 'Monologue Notes is here' — direct product launch CTA
MID: 'Voice notes are the most underrated input method for creative work' — domain thesis without CTA
FT: 'Seeing Monologue with Dia and Granola feels great!' — 82 likes, 10 replies. Zero ask. Maximum vibe.
AST: 'Dan's podcast on voice AI is a masterclass' — boosting editorial + reinforcing Monologue's domain
AIR: Near-zero airball rate. Naveen rarely misses.
twitter.walkway_notes: Naveen's free throw percentage is the team average. His cultural tweets (Dia and Granola, life updates) build trust at scale. When he does shoot a 3 (product launch), it lands because he's already made 10 free throws that week. The walkway is: cultural trust → product curiosity → usage. Naveen proves that you can be a product GM and still be a person first.

linkedin.opportunity: LinkedIn is where voice-note productivity could find enterprise traction. Sales teams, executives, journalists — they all need voice capture. Naveen's personal brand is warm enough to soften the enterprise pitch.

instagram.opportunity: Monologue is about VOICE. Instagram Stories with voice notes as the medium would be meta-marketing. 'A day in the life of a voice-note founder' — the product IS the content.

product_hunt.opportunity: Monologue is on PH. Naveen's cultural warmth would translate to PH comments and community building. Maker profiles humanize products.

surface_iq_summary.superpower: Cultural FT% — trust compounds in public, converts in private
surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Naveen's FT% is elite. He rarely shoots 3s, but when he does, they land. The catalyst isn't 'post more promos' — it's 'recognize that your cultural tweets are doing the heavy lifting.' The growth loop: trust compounds in public, converts in private.
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: You can be a product GM and still be a person. Naveen's ratio of 'life' to 'product' is the model for sustainable public presence. The team learns: free throws make 3s easier.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: INSTAGRAM STORIES: Monologue is a voice product. Voice-note Stories showing 'how I use Monologue to capture ideas' would be the most authentic marketing possible. The product IS the content format.

growth_loop_position.attribution_mechanism: Cultural warmth → product curiosity → voice-note usage. Naveen's humanity is Monologue's brand.
growth_loop_position.compound_insight: Naveen's 'Dia and Granola' tweet has a longer half-life than his product launch tweet. People remember the person; the product follows.

notable_shots: 3P: 'Subscribe to Every for the best writing on AI and business' — rare direct CTA, usually in bio
MID: 'The edit is where the essay becomes itself' — writing craft expertise that proves editorial authority
FT: 'Personal news: moving to a new city, terrified and excited' — vulnerability that builds reader trust
AST: 'Laura's piece on AI and journalism is essential reading' — boosting team editorial + reinforcing EIC role
AIR: Rare — Kate's editorial instinct keeps quality high
twitter.walkway_notes: Kate's writing craft tweets aren't 'growth content.' They're credibility content. When the Editor in Chief of Every talks about how editing works, she creates the trust that makes Every's paid editorial product worth subscribing to. She's not selling the newsletter. She's demonstrating the expertise that the newsletter contains. The walkway: editorial craft → publication authority → subscription value.

linkedin.opportunity: LinkedIn is where media industry credibility lives. Kate's editorial perspective on 'the future of publishing' would attract the exact audience that values Every's long-form authority.

instagram.opportunity: Not a visual surface for Kate. Skip.

product_hunt.opportunity: Not a primary PH surface for editorial. But: 'Every' as a publication could have a PH presence for newsletter discovery. Unconventional but worth testing.

surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Kate's mid-range game is her superpower. She never shoots 3s (promo) but she's always open for the elbow jumper (credibility). That consistency is what makes the subscription funnel work. The catalyst: turn her editorial craft threads into a recurring format — 'How We Edited This Week's Cover Story.'
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: Credibility before conversion. Show the process, not just the product. The GMs can learn from Kate: 'How I edit' is more compelling than 'Subscribe to Every' because it proves the value before asking for the transaction.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: PODCAST: A 10-minute 'Editor's Note' audio segment would be the most authentic marketing asset Every has. Kate's voice explaining why an essay matters would convert subscribers who skim but don't read.

growth_loop_position.attribution_mechanism: Editorial craft demonstration → publication authority → newsletter subscription. Kate's expertise IS the product promise.
growth_loop_position.compound_insight: Kate's editing philosophy tweets have a 12-month half-life. Writers bookmark them, reference them in their own work, and eventually subscribe to Every because they trust the editorial standard.

instagram.opportunity: Katie's personal voice (baking, creative life) is perfect for Instagram Stories. The 'writer's life' aesthetic would build the audience that eventually reads Every.

surface_iq_summary.superpower: Transition Game — personal relatability pushes the pace toward creative credibility
surface_iq_summary.catalyst_opportunity: Katie's FT% is higher than her 3P% — which is exactly right for a writer. Her personal tweets create the audience that reads her professional work. The catalyst: don't rush the 3. Let the transition game create the fast break.
surface_iq_summary.teachable_moment: Personal relatability is not off-topic. For writers, the personal IS the professional. The team learns: your life creates the audience that your work converts.
surface_iq_summary.absent_surface_opportunity: INSTAGRAM: 'A writer's life' content — morning routines, editing process, baking breaks — would build the lifestyle audience that Every's editorial voice resonates with.

instagram.opportunity: Not a primary surface. Laura's strength is industry analysis, not lifestyle content.

product_hunt.opportunity: Same as Katie — unconventional for writers, but PH newsletters are an emerging category. Laura's industry analysis could find a PH audience.
